title: Causal Degradation Acceleration Features
summary: F1 tyre performance can degrade nonlinearly, with pace loss accelerating before a stop.
rationale: F1 tyre performance can degrade nonlinearly, with pace loss accelerating before a stop. Current strong features use cumulative degradation and ratios, but not causal within-stint slope/curvature of degradation and lap-time loss.
implementation_hint: Within Race_Year/Driver/Stint sorted by LapNumber, compute rolling causal slopes and second differences for LapTime_Delta and Cumulative_Degradation, early-stint baseline z-scores, and cliff flags. Winsorize pit/out-lap anomalies before slope calculations.
expected_effect: Should identify tyres approaching a performance cliff before static tyre-life windows trigger.
risk: If lap-time features are heavily synthetic or noisy, rolling derivatives may add variance.
